blocks.text: DTC P0139: Secondary O 2 Abnormal sensor characteristics

Secondary O 2 The sensor detects the oxygen concentration downstream of the catalytic converter during the theoretical AFR feedback control based on the output voltage of the LAF sensor; the PGM-FI ECU ensures that the purification efficiency of the catalytic converter is optimal by using a secondary O 2 The air-fuel ratio is controlled from the sensor and LAF sensor output voltage. Secondary O 2 When the throttle valve is closed after the sensor heater is energized, the secondary O 2 If the sensor output voltage stays within the set range and does not change to the lean A/F region, the PGM-FI ECU determines a failure and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status

rows.text: Detection order (of the DTC in question)
State in which P0138 is determined to be normal
2D/C (2 consecutive detection method), PGM-FI warning light: On
Normal judgment, failure judgment, running, out of condition
Engine intake air temperature [Intake air temperature sensor]
Secondary O 2 Sensor output voltage [Secondary O 2 SENSOR]
DTC requesting the prohibition of detection of the relevant DTC

blocks.text: Secondary O 2 Sensor output voltage [secondary O 2 sensor] is above 0.05V for more than 10 seconds.

items.text: Secondary O 2 Sensor responsiveness degradation failure
Start the engine, run the radiator fan twice, and then warm up the engine for at least 2 minutes at 3,000 rpm with no load.
Drive at a vehicle speed [vehicle speed] of 40-60 km/h for at least 15 minutes.
Decelerate from a vehicle speed of 60 km/h for at least 10 seconds with the throttle valve fully closed.
If the deceleration time cannot be secured due to the driving environment, a test run shall be conducted by changing the gearshift state, driving location, etc.
When conducting test drives, the driver must comply with all laws and regulations, observe good manners, and drive in accordance with the surrounding conditions.

blocks.text: If the vehicle is determined to have a fault, a temporary DTC is stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory. If the same failure is detected at the next D/C (two consecutive detections), the PGM-FI warning light comes on and the DTC and freeze data are stored.

If the vehicle is diagnosed and determined to be normal after running consecutive 3D/C runs, the PGM-FI warning light will turn off. In addition, the PGM-FI warning light, temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are cleared by using the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or by removing the 12V battery terminal.
